business.headline: Gold, silver prices to stay volatile. Should you buy or avoid?Gold is expected to trade between Rs 144,000 and Rs 154,000 per 10 gm, while silver could move in the Rs 215,000 to Rs 243,000 per kg range before a breakout or breakdown.

What every Indian must understand before buying a life insurance policyLife Insurance is not merely a financial product; it is a safeguard for the people who depend on you. Understanding its purpose, structure and role in financial planning can make the difference between simply owning a policy and truly protecting your family.

From oil prices to war zones: Why global risks shape your investments nowAt the centre of the recent turmoil lies the Strait of Hormuz — a narrow maritime corridor that carries nearly 20 million barrels of oil daily, roughly one-fifth of global consumption.

Govt doubles states' quota of 5 kg LPG cylinders meant for migrant workersIn an order issued on April 6, the government said the daily quantity of 5 kg free trade LPG (FTL) cylinders available for distribution to migrant labourers will be doubled, based on the average daily number of cylinders supplied on March 2 and 3, 2026.

Nayara to shut Vadinar refinery from April 9; will fuel supply take a hit?The timing of this shutdown is important. Ongoing tensions in the Middle East, including the US-Iran conflict, have already tightened the availability of oil and gas globally. This has put pressure on imports of crude oil, natural gas and LPG into India.

Sensex jumps 787 points: 3 reasons why stock market took a U-turn and rose todayStock market rally: The biggest trigger for the recovery was news of a possible Iran-US ceasefire framework. This comes after a weak start to the day, where markets were under pressure due to global tensions.

RBI MPC begins under shadow of global conflict: What it means for your moneyThe RBI MPC meeting comes at a difficult moment, as global tensions and rising oil prices have made the central bank's job more complex. The repo rate currently stands at 5.25%.

entertainment.headline: Disha Vakani's father, Gujarati actor Bhim Vakani, dies at 84 TV actor Disha Vakani's father and renowned playwright Bhim Vakani died at 84 after battling paralysis and pneumonia.

Anupam Kher calls comedian Samay Raina outstanding at 28, shares life lessons Actor Anupam Kher shared a heartfelt note after meeting comedian Samay Raina and his parents at his acting school. 9 songs in 9 days: Aditya Dhar recalls Shashwat Sachdev's Dhurandhar music blitz Filmmaker Aditya Dhar shared how his home turned into a living studio during the making of Dhurandhar's songs. He praised Shashwat Sachdev's speed, passion and dedication behind the film's chart-topping music.

Tabu revisits old dance class days with Akshay Kumar: He picked me up on a bike Akshay Kumar and Tabu opened up about their long-standing bond during Bhooth Bangla promotions. From bike rides together to his unchanged habits, they shared fond memories from their early days.

Tovino Thomas calls Lokah 2 biggest challenge in his career. Here's why Malayalam actor Tovino Thomas shared details about the challenging Lokah 2 project, highlighting its complexity and scale. The sequel's production is expected to start later this year, following the success of Lokah Chapter 1.

Chand Mera Dil teaser: Ananya, Lakshya's film offers campus love and heartbreak The Chand Mera Dil teaser showcases Ananya Panday and Lakshya's intense romance, hinting at a story of love, loss and second chances.

Today's fiction quota? Trisha hits back at retirement rumours with witty post Rumours have been rife lately on social media that actor Trisha Krishnan is quitting cinema. The actor on Tuesday responded to the speculations with a witty post on Instagram.

Sushant Singh Rajput as Lord Ram? Sister Shweta reacts to viral AI images in moving post Shweta Singh Kirti reacted to viral AI images imagining brother and late actor Sushant Singh Rajput as Lord Ram. Her emotional note has struck a chord with fans amid the buzz around Ramayana.

Sunil Grover's Javed Akhtar act wins internet, leaves Raj Babbar in splits Sunil Grover's mimicry of Javed Akhtar at the Screen Awards 2026 has once again won the internet, leaving the audience in splits including the legendary poet himself.

If Jana Nayagan waited, why not TN 2026: Plea against new Tamil film before polls A plea has been filed in Madras High Court to stall the release of Tamil political thriller titled TN 2026 before Tamil Nadu Assembly elections. The petitioner alleged that the film may influence voters and violate election laws, while invoking Jana Nayagan delay.

This means everything: Aditya Dhar reacts to Virat-Anushka's Dhurandhar 2 review Aditya Dhar reacted to glowing praise from Virat Kohli and Anushka Sharma for Dhurandhar: The Revenge. The filmmaker called the moment "truly overwhelming" as the film continues its record-breaking run.

Pool time, video call with Malti, Nick: Priyanka's off day amid Varanasi shoot Priyanka Chopra shares glimpses of a relaxing Sunday in Hyderabad amid her Varanasi shoot. From poolside moments to family time with Nick Jonas and Malti, her post delighted fans.

TV actor Subashini's last Instagram post shows serene moments before death A day after Subashini Balasubramaniyam was found dead in Chennai, her final Instagram post has resurfaced online. The video, shared just two days earlier, has left fans emotional.

Moreover, little clarity on why the US destroyed its own aircraft has only added to the intrigue. Trump's narrative of the sequence of events has left several unanswered questions.

But with decades of delays, cost overruns and repeated setbacks for the LCA Tejas, has Hindustan Aeronautics Limited (HAL) spent years and thousands of crores on a dud?

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

Mojtaba Khamenei unconscious, not involved in decision-making: ReportThe Times report said that Mojtaba Khamenei sustained injuries in the same US-Israeli attacks that killed Ayatollah Ali Khameni and is presently undergoing treatment in a private hospital in the city of Qom.

Watch: Moon captured like never before as Artemis 2 makes historic fly-byA 40-minute blackout, then a view that feels unreal. As Artemis 2 re-emerges from the Moon's far side, a viral video shared by Elon Musk is giving the world a glimpse few have ever seen.

2 children dead, parents critical in Ahmedabad after eating dosaAccording to Gaurishankar Prajapti, the grandfather of the two deceased girls, his son Vimal bought the khiru from a nearby shop named Ghanashyam Dairy to make dosa. The next morning, Vimal, his wife Bhavna, and two of his daughters started vomiting and were taken to the hospital as their conditions worsened.

Live: Airstrike on Iran's Alborz province residential area kills at least 18, injures 24US-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: US President Donald Trump has escalated tensions with Iran, warning that "power plants and bridges" could be targeted if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened by Tuesday evening, saying "Tuesday will be Power Plant Day, and Bridge Day, all wrapped up in one." Trump's abusive threat drew a sharp from Iranian parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, who warned that Trump's "reckless moves" risk dragging the country into living hell and insisting "you won't gain anything through war crimes." Behind the scenes, Oman has held urgent talks with Iran to ensure smooth flow of transit through war-hit Hormuz, while Pakistan and Egypt have kept diplomatic channels open between Washington and Tehran. At the same time, violence has intensified elsewhere, with Israeli strikes in Beirut killing at least four people and Hezbollah launching retaliatory attacks, escalating the conflict further. Follow IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

Fake paneer, real scandal? Food regulator FSSAI in eye of an FIR stormFood adulteration is a public health crisis in India and the FSSAI, the country's food safety regulator, has faced incessant attacks over its inaction. The FSSAI is facing a fresh storm over an FIR filed against activists who alleged irregularities in its hiring of top officials.

Live: Not every issue is gender-based, government tells Supreme CourtSabarimala SC Hearing Live Updates: A nine-judge bench of the Supreme Court will begin final hearings from April 7 on petitions concerning discrimination against women at religious places, including the Sabarimala temple, and the scope of religious freedom. The Constitution bench is led by Chief Justice Surya Kant. The case stems from a 2018 verdict, in which the top court, by a 4-1 majority, allowed entry of women aged 10 to 50 into the Sabarimala shrine, calling the ban unconstitutional. In 2019, a separate five-judge bench referred broader questions on women's entry and religious freedom across faiths to a larger bench for consideration. The Centre and the Kerala government have backed pleas for a review of the 2018 verdict. Stay with Indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

Ever wondered why you get goosebumps? There is hidden science behind itIn this week's edition of The Daily Why, find out why you can feel a chill at the back of your neck or the tiny hair on your arms stand up when you feel cold or scared or excited.

Indian student drowns in New Zealand during Easter outing with friendsA 21-year-old Indian student from Mumbai, Shaurya Sriram, drowned in a lake in New Zealand during an Easter holiday trip with friends. The student from the University of Auckland was pulled from the lake after struggling in deeper waters. He was unconscious when rescued and could not be revived.

Don't use train for next 12 hours for sake of your security, Israel warns IraniansA late-night warning from Israel has raised fresh concerns, asking people across Iran to avoid trains and railway lines, hinting at potential risks as tensions in the region continue to escalate.

This comes amid reports that minors as young as 12 have been deployed at Basij checkpoints, which have been targeted in strikes.

sports.headline: Vaishnavi Adkar crowned India's new tennis No.1 with sensational run in 2026 Vaishnavi Adkar's dream 2026 run has taken her to the top of Indian women's singles, with the 21-year-old climbing from World No. 643 to No.383 to become the country's new No.1. She is currently taking part in the Billie Jean King Cup 2026 in New Delhi.

His comments come even as attention slowly builds towards the 2027 ODI World Cup.

Shreyas Iyer's sister not holding back, trolls KKR after washout in Kolkata: Watch IPL 2026: Shreyas Iyer's sister, Shresta, went viral after cheekily trolling Kolkata Knight Riders with an Instagram reel following the rain-hit washout of their clash against Punjab Kings at Eden Gardens.

Hetmyer's form not a concern for RR: Coach backs batter to rise from slump IPL 2026: RR have downplayed concerns over Shimron Hetmyer's slow start to IPL 2026, backing the West Indies star to come good soon. Performance coach Siddhartha Lahiri stressed that it is too early to judge, insisting the team remains confident in its middle-order strength.

Tim David reveals RCB drill behind 106m six vs CSK: We compete to hit the roof IPL 2026: Tim David revealed RCB's unique roof-hitting drills behind his 106m six vs CSK. The Aussie powered RCB to a massive total with a stunning late assault.

How Jasprit Bumrah, MI can stop RR teen sensation IPL 2026, RR vs MI: Ex-Australia skipper Aaron Finch believes Jasprit Bumrah should target Vaibhav Sooryavanshi early with fuller deliveries and swing when Mumbai Indians face Rajasthan Royals on Tuesday at the Barsapara Cricket Stadium in Guwahati.

KKR woes deepen: Pathirana still awaiting IPL clearance from Sri Lanka IPL 2026: KKR's bowling woes continue as Matheesha Pathirana awaits IPL clearance. The Sri Lankan pacer could join the squad only by mid-April.

Mumbai Indians play down Vaibhav Sooryavanshi hype, Ravi Bishnoi no mystery RR vs MI, IPL 2026: Mumbai Indians play down Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's hype ahead of RR clash, with Paras Mhambrey saying there are no special plans, while also dismissing Ravi Bishnoi as a mystery threat.

Green not good enough? Pietersen's scathing take on KKR's misfiring star Kevin Pietersen delivered a blunt take on Cameron Green after the Australian all-rounder continued to struggle in the IPL 2026 campaign. Green scored just four runs during the match against PBKS.

Graceful in defeat, Sheetal Devi helps limbless archer Payal Nag on podium: VideoSheetal Devi showed rare grace in defeat, helping limbless archer Payal Nag face the flag on the podium. The teenager from Odisha had just stunned the world number one to win gold in the World Archery Para Series in Bangkok.

Heavenly intervention: Rain washes away KKR's toss blunder vs PBKS in KolkataIPL 2026, KKR vs PBKS: Kolkata Knight Riders were reeling at 25 for 2 in the fourth over when rain intervened, forcing a no-result in Kolkata. Ajinkya Rahane's decision to win the toss and opt to bat under overcast skies drew sharp criticism.

Rahane justified his call to bat first, but not everyone was convinced by the decision.

Will MI captain Hardik Pandya be ready for RR clash? Bowling coach offers updateMumbai Indians have offered an update on Hardik Pandya after the all-rounder missed the loss against Delhi Capitals. Hardik missed the game due to illness.

PSL to beat IPL? The financial reality behind Mohsin Naqvi's No. 1 league claimPCB Chairman Mohsin Naqvi claims the PSL will soon become the most sought-after T20 league in cricket. However, with the IPL's $18.5 billion valuation dwarfing the PSL's $260 million, the financial reality suggests a massive divide.

KKR vs PBKS Highlights: Rain saves Kolkata's blushes as match called offKolkata (KKR) vs Punjab (PBKS) Highlights, IPL 2026: A batting blunder from Kolkata has been saved by rain as the match at the Eden Gardens against Punjab gets called off. Having won the toss, KKR opted to bat first in a bizarre call and were made to pay as they were reeling at 25 for 2 in 3.4 overs. Rain was relentless for a good portion of the night and despite relenting at the end, the match had to be called off. Both teams get one point each in the end.

RCB star gets Dinesh Karthik's backing for India call-up: Difficult to keep him outIPL 2026: Royal Challengers Bengaluru mentor Dinesh Karthik heaped praise on Devdutt Padikkal's rich vein of form and leadership qualities, saying the 25-year-old left-hander will push for a place in the Indian T20I side. Padikkal smashed a fifty in RCB's 43-run win over Chennai Super Kings on Sunday.

IPL 2026: Rashid Khan is developing a secret deliveryGujarat Titans spinner Rashid Khan is developing a secret delivery. Speaking to India Today, Rashid revealed that he has been forced to experiment due to the rapid evolution of batters in the last two years in the league.

While the women's section is heating up thanks to Vaishali's surge, Praggnanandhaa has been outpaced by the brilliance of Javokhir Sindarov in the Open category.

With the star doubles duo injured, the spotlight shifts to Lakshya Sen and PV Sindhu to carry the nation's hopes and end a decades-long wait for a singles title.

Meanwhile, there is also a threat of rain, just as when the two teams met last year in Kolkata.

Former cricketer Ambati Rayudu revealed that CSK had been trying this bizarre tactics in training for years, but were not able to implement it due to the presence of MS Dhoni.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
